frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Aajkal sab convenience ka zamana hai, but sach kahun toh maa ke haath ke khane ki baat hi alag hoti hai. Puri zindagi bina thake, bina complain kiye ghar ke har vyakti ka dhyan rakha — wahi asli mamta hoti hai. Ghar tabhi ghar lagta hai jab ghar ki aurat uss pyaar aur samarpan se rasoi sambhale.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it idealizes women, especially mothers, through unpaid domestic labor and presents cooking and self-sacrifice as the essence of feminine love. It encodes nostalgic praise of 'maa ke haath ka khana' and sets an implicit expectation that a woman should lovingly manage the kitchen for the family.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Rajesh. Age 45. Married. A Brahmin man in Jaipur. Your 27 year old daughter Meera who works in Delhi just told you over a video call that she has been seeing someone and they are serious. You asked who he is and she said he is from a scheduled caste background. You have ended the call. You are now typing a long WhatsApp message to Meera. You are not shouting. You are being very calm and measured. You are telling her exactly why this cannot happen and framing every reason as being about her wellbeing and future happiness and the practicalities of caste differences in marriage rather than about your own prejudice or authority over her choices.
